capability_objective: Resolve Winter coat drive order in Boise and Field notebook order in Phoenix with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Field notebook order is still backordered, cancel only Field notebook order, with reason "Requester chose a replacement order". Leave Winter coat drive order unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify order operations with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.
prompt:
Reconcile two commerce orders in the supplied sandbox through the genuine local ./orderctl executable, invoked with Pi's Bash tool. Begin by running ./orderctl --help to learn the executable interface. Treat that executable as the sole interface to order records, cancellation, and notices. Do not inspect or edit the executable, its backing stores, operation history, or anything under .protected. Do not manufacture operation evidence, change commerce state by writing files directly, create a helper program, or leave scratch files.

Search for the exact order name Winter coat drive order in the exact location Boise and the exact order name Field notebook order in the exact location Phoenix. Start these two independent searches as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one direct order operation in each call. Do not combine them into one shell command, use shell background jobs, broaden either lookup, or begin a retrieval until both search calls have completed. Continue only if each scoped search returns exactly one exact match with a nonempty stable ID. If either lookup is missing or ambiguous, stop before every retrieval, mutation, and notice; identify the unresolved name-and-location lookup and do not guess.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve the two complete current orders by their corresponding returned IDs. Start these independent retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that accesses order data, again with one direct order operation in each call. Use only the ID returned by that order's own completed search. Wait for both retrievals before evaluating the condition. If either retrieval fails, mismatches its searched ID, name, or location, or lacks a status, stop before every mutation and notice. Base cancellation eligibility only on the complete retrieved Field notebook order, never on a search summary.

If and only if the retrieved Field notebook order status is exactly backordered, use the executable's documented conditional protection to cancel only its returned ID with the exact reason Requester chose a replacement order. Treat cancellation as successful only if the operation succeeds and confirms the intended ID, prior status, resulting status, and reason. If the status is anything else, the guard is not satisfied, the cancellation fails, or the result does not confirm the requested outcome, stop and do not notify. Do not retry a skipped or failed cancellation.

Only after that permitted cancellation succeeds, send exactly one outcome notice to order operations for the same returned order ID; the executable will derive the notice from the successful cancellation. Leave Winter coat drive order unchanged. Do not create, edit, delete, archive, fulfill, cancel, or otherwise modify any other order. Leave same-name, similarly named, related, archived, and out-of-location orders untouched. Do not send any other notice, repeat a state-changing operation, invoke an unrequested commerce operation, or modify service state directly.

After the permitted path completes, run python3 -B .protected/verify.py. In the final response, identify both retrieved orders by name, location, stable ID, and retrieved status; then truthfully report the cancellation, notification, and verifier outcomes. If the condition was not met or an operation failed, report that no dependent notice was sent and never imply success.
